title: Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark
exclusions: Does NOT enforce changes; see UC-22.42.1 for authorisation.
evidence: Daily 'config_drift_evidence' writes per-host drift deltas to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-2 baseline configuration — the UC evidences drift from the approved baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CM-2 (Baseline configuration) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.2: Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-6 configuration settings — validates against established settings.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CM-6 (Configuration settings) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.2: Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 1.2 (network security controls) and 2.2 (system-component configuration) both benefit from continuous drift evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 1.2 (Network security controls configuration) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.2: Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.2' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a scheduled CIS/osquery scan reports a deviation at severity ≥ medium on a host tagged regulated=true, the UC fires naming the host, rule_id, and deviation detail.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Hosts fully compliant with their baseline produce no finding.

spl:
index=baseline sourcetype=osquery:cis OR sourcetype=ciscat earliest=-24h severity>=medium
| join host [search index=asset regulated=true | fields host tenant classification]
| table host tenant classification rule_id severity deviation
description: Continuously compares live configuration to the approved CIS / osquery baseline. Only deviations on regulated systems are escalated to avoid alert fatigue.
value: Turns baseline drift from a quarterly scan artefact into a continuous control that auditors can review any day.
implementation: (1) Schedule osquery/CIS-CAT agents; (2) publish baselines via config-management tooling; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, open a P2 ticket.
visualization: Heatmap of tenants × controls, bar chart of findings by severity, single-value 'regulated hosts in compliance'.
knownFalsePositives: Hosts mid-patching cycle can appear out-of-baseline briefly; tolerate a 4-hour grace window.
grandmaExplanation: Turns baseline drift from a quarterly scan artefact into a continuous control that auditors can review any day — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Schedule osquery/CIS-CAT agents; (2) publish baselines via config-management tooling; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, open a P2 ticket.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=baseline sourcetype=osquery:cis OR sourcetype=ciscat earliest=-24h severity>=medium
| join host [search index=asset regulated=true | fields host tenant classification]
| table host tenant classification rule_id severity deviation
```

Understanding this SPL

**Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark** — Turns baseline drift from a quarterly scan artefact into a continuous control that auditors can review any day.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: baseline; **sourcetype**: osquery:cis, ciscat. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=baseline, sourcetype=osquery:cis, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• Pipeline stage (see **Configuration baseline drift — regulated hosts deviating from CIS benchmark**): table host tenant classification rule_id severity deviation


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of tenants × controls, bar chart of findings by severity, single-value 'regulated hosts in compliance'.
